questions.q: In which House of Parliament is the ceremonial mace used as a symbol of authority?
questions.explanation: The mace is the symbol of authority of the Lok Sabha and its presiding officer.

questions.q: The Lok Sabha mace symbolises the authority of which official in particular?
options: The presiding officer of the House
questions.explanation: The mace represents the authority of the presiding officer and of the House itself.

questions.q: Where is the mace placed when the Lok Sabha is in session?
questions.explanation: The mace is placed near the Speaker's chair while the House is in session.

questions.q: By what name is the ceremonial sceptre placed in the new Parliament building known?
questions.explanation: The ceremonial sceptre installed in the new Parliament is known as the Sengol.

questions.q: What broadly is a mace, as used in the Lok Sabha?
questions.explanation: A mace is a ceremonial staff carried as an emblem of authority.

questions.q: How does the mace usually arrive in the chamber at the start of a sitting?
options: It is projected on a screen
questions.explanation: The mace is carried into the chamber ceremonially when the House assembles.

questions.q: The Lok Sabha is also known by which name?
questions.explanation: The Lok Sabha, whose authority the mace represents, is the House of the People.

questions.q: The mace is primarily a symbol of which of the following?
questions.explanation: The mace stands as a symbol of the authority of the House and its presiding officer.

questions.q: Removing or seizing the mace during proceedings is generally treated as what?
options: A grave breach of House decorum
questions.explanation: Disturbing the mace is regarded as a serious breach of the dignity of the House.

questions.q: The Sengol installed in the new Parliament is most directly associated with which Indian region's tradition?
questions.explanation: The Sengol draws on a Tamil ceremonial tradition of a sceptre signifying just rule.

questions.q: In which year was the new Parliament building, where the Sengol was installed, inaugurated?
questions.explanation: The new Parliament building was inaugurated in 2023, when the Sengol was installed.

questions.q: Near whose chair in the chamber is the Sengol placed in the new Parliament?
questions.explanation: The Sengol is installed close to the Speaker's chair, like the mace, marking the seat of authority.

questions.q: The word 'Sengol' is derived from a term meaning which of the following?
questions.explanation: The term Sengol is associated with the idea of righteousness and just governance.

questions.q: The mace of a legislature in the Westminster tradition historically signifies the authority of which body?
options: The legislature and its presiding officer
questions.explanation: In the Westminster tradition a mace represents the authority of the legislature and its presiding officer.

questions.q: When proceedings of the Lok Sabha are formally adjourned, what typically happens to the mace?
options: It is kept permanently on display outside
It is handed to the Prime Minister
It is removed from its place near the chair
It is taken to the Rajya Sabha
questions.explanation: The mace is placed during sittings and removed when the House is not in session.

questions.q: The presiding officer whose authority the Lok Sabha mace represents is which official?
options: The Chairman of the Rajya Sabha
questions.explanation: The Speaker is the presiding officer of the Lok Sabha, whose authority the mace symbolises.

questions.q: Which of these best describes the role of the mace during a Lok Sabha sitting?
options: It marks the House as duly constituted and in session
It measures the time of debates
questions.explanation: The placed mace signifies that the House is properly constituted and conducting business.

questions.q: Items such as the mace and the Sengol fall into which broad category of parliamentary heritage?
questions.explanation: The mace and Sengol are ceremonial regalia symbolising the dignity and authority of the House.

questions.q: The placement of a sceptre or mace near the seat of authority is meant chiefly to convey which idea?
questions.explanation: Such regalia symbolise the legitimate authority vested in the office and the House.

questions.q: In which city are the Lok Sabha mace and the Sengol housed?
questions.explanation: Parliament and its regalia are located in New Delhi, the seat of the national legislature.

questions.q: The mace as a symbol of legislative authority entered Indian practice largely through which influence?
questions.explanation: The use of a mace in the legislature derives from the British Westminster parliamentary tradition.

questions.q: Which statement about the mace's function is most accurate?
options: It has no fixed place in the chamber
It is a symbolic, not a legislative, instrument
It is required to pass every law
It replaces the Speaker when absent
questions.explanation: The mace is a symbolic emblem of authority and plays no direct part in lawmaking itself.

questions.q: The Sengol installed in 2023 is historically linked to which moment in Indian history?
questions.explanation: The Sengol is associated with the transfer of power at the time of independence in August 1947.

questions.q: The Tamil ceremonial sceptre tradition behind the Sengol is most closely connected with which historical institution?
questions.explanation: The sceptre tradition reflected in the Sengol is associated with the Chola dynasty of the Tamil region.

questions.q: A figure of which animal commonly surmounts the Sengol, echoing a sacred Indian motif?
questions.explanation: The Sengol is surmounted by a figure of Nandi, the bull, a sacred motif in the tradition it draws upon.

questions.q: Before its installation in the new Parliament, the historic Sengol had for decades been kept in which type of institution?
questions.explanation: The historic Sengol had been preserved in a museum collection before being installed in Parliament.

questions.q: Which principle is the Sengol intended to convey to the holder of authority?
options: A duty to rule justly and righteously
questions.explanation: The Sengol traditionally symbolises a charge to govern with justice and righteousness.

questions.q: Within the chamber, both the mace and the Sengol are best understood as performing which kind of role?
questions.explanation: Both objects serve a ceremonial and symbolic function rather than any procedural lawmaking role.

questions.q: The dignity attached to the mace means that any act disturbing it during a sitting is dealt with by which authority?
options: The presiding officer of the House
questions.explanation: Maintaining the dignity of the House, including respect for the mace, falls to the presiding officer.

questions.q: The installation of the Sengol alongside the parliamentary mace reflects which broad theme?
options: A change to the voting age
A blending of indigenous and parliamentary symbols of authority
A reform of the tax system
A new method of counting votes
questions.explanation: Placing the Sengol in Parliament blends an indigenous symbol of just rule with the inherited parliamentary tradition.
